name: 2D Shapes and Their Properties
whatIsIt: Flat shapes are described by their SIDES and CORNERS: a triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners, a rectangle has 4 sides with opposite sides equal, a square is a special rectangle with ALL sides equal, and a circle has no corners at all — one smooth curved edge. Naming shapes means counting and comparing their parts.
whyNeeded: Everything built has a shape: windows, roofs, wheels, kites, tiles, road signs. Builders, tailors, and artists talk in shapes. Knowing the properties (not just the names) lets you SPOT shapes anywhere — even squashed, tilted, or upside down!
realLifeUses: Road signs: triangles warn, circles order, rectangles inform
Home: rectangular doors, square tiles, circular clocks and plates
Kites and samosas: triangles you can eat and fly!
Carrom board and chess board: squares inside squares

vocabulary.meaning: One straight edge of a shape.
4 equal sides, 4 square corners — a special rectangle!
4 sides, opposite sides equal, 4 square corners.

story.title: Shapes in the Festival Kites
story.text: Ravi's uncle makes festival kites of every shape. 'Which one has 3 sides and 3 corners?' he asks. Ravi points: 'The triangle kite!' 'And which has NO corners at all?' 'The round one — the circle, one smooth curve.' 'Exactly! Sides and corners are how we tell shapes apart,' says his uncle.
story.extractedProblem: Which shape has 3 sides and 3 corners, and which has no corners at all?
story.answerInStory: The triangle has 3 sides and 3 corners; the circle has no corners — one smooth curve.

visual.caption: Count sides and corners to name a shape. The square is a special rectangle whose sides are ALL equal.

teachingGallery.title: Name a shape by its sides and corners
teachingGallery.note: Count the straight sides — that tells you the name.

examples.caption: No corners, one curved edge → circle.

standardMethod.summary: To name a shape: count sides, count corners, compare side lengths.
steps: Compare sides: all equal? opposite pairs equal?
3 sides → triangle. 4 sides + opposite equal → rectangle. 4 sides ALL equal → square. No sides, one curve → circle.

alternateMethods.name: Shape hunt (real-world spotting)
alternateMethods.whenToUse: To burn shapes into memory using your own house.
steps: Pick a room and find 3 things per shape.
Trace each one's edges with your finger, counting aloud.
Tilted or big or small — the count never lies!
alternateMethods.example: Door → rectangle (count 4 corners), plate → circle, samosa → triangle.

workedExamples.problem: A shape has 4 sides. Its top and bottom are 5 cm; its left and right are 3 cm. Name it.
steps: 4 sides with square corners → rectangle family.
Opposite sides equal but not all → a rectangle (not a square).

workedExamples.problem: Ravi says a square turned 45° (standing on its corner like a diamond) 'is not a square any more.' Is he right?
steps: Turning a shape changes NOTHING about its sides and corners.
Count: still 4 equal sides, still 4 square corners.
It is still a square — just tilted! Names come from properties, not poses.
workedExamples.answer: Still a square — turning doesn't change properties.

trickPractice.intro: The number of straight sides names the shape — 3 triangle, 5 pentagon, 6 hexagon.

questions.q: A shape with 3 straight sides is a…
A shape with 5 sides is a…
A shape with 6 sides is a…
A round shape with no corners is a…

commonMistakes.mistake: Thinking a tilted square becomes a 'diamond' and stops being a square.
commonMistakes.fix: Shapes keep their names in every pose! Count sides and corners — if all 4 sides are equal with square corners, it's a square, however it stands.

commonMistakes.mistake: Saying a square is NOT a rectangle.
commonMistakes.fix: A square passes every rectangle test (4 sides, opposite sides equal, square corners) — it's a SPECIAL rectangle where all sides happen to be equal. Every square is a rectangle; not every rectangle is a square!

commonMistakes.mistake: Saying a circle has 1 side or counting curves as straight sides.
commonMistakes.fix: Sides are STRAIGHT edges. A circle has no straight edges and no corners — just one smooth curve all the way around.

easy.q: Which shape has exactly 3 sides and 3 corners?
hintLadder: Count the sides in each shape's name-picture.
Three sides, three corners — the roof shape.

easy.q: How many corners does a rectangle have?

medium.q: All 4 sides of a shape are equal, and its corners are square. What is it?

options.label: A square (and it's also a special rectangle!)
Only a rectangle, never a square

medium.answer: A square (and it's also a special rectangle!)
hintLadder: ALL sides equal is the special clue.
It also passes the rectangle test — special rectangle!

medium.q: Geo tilts a square to stand on one corner. Ravi shouts 'It became a diamond — new shape!' What does Geo say?

options.label: Count the sides — still 4 equal sides, still a square!
Ravi is right — tilting changes the shape

medium.answer: Count the sides — still 4 equal sides, still a square!
hintLadder: Does turning change the number of sides?
Do the sides shrink or grow when tilted?

challenge.q: TRUE or FALSE: every square is a rectangle.

options.label: True — a square passes every rectangle test

challenge.answer: True — a square passes every rectangle test
hintLadder: List the rectangle rules: 4 sides, opposite sides equal, square corners.
Does a square break any of them?
No — it just adds one MORE rule (all equal).

challenge.q: Geo builds a hexagon window. How many corners does it have?
hintLadder: Six sides always means six corners.

questions.q: How many sides does a triangle have?
What makes a square SPECIAL among rectangles?
How many corners does a circle have?
hintLadder: Does a circle have any straight edges?

options.label: Still a rectangle — properties don't change with pose
A new shape called a slanty

questions.answer: Still a rectangle — properties don't change with pose
hintLadder: Count sides and corners after tilting.

teachBackPrompt: Be Geo's junior architect! Show someone at home one triangle, one square, one rectangle, and one circle IN your house, and prove each name by counting sides and corners aloud.

revisionCard.summary: Name shapes by properties: triangle 3 sides/corners; rectangle 4 sides, opposite equal; square = special rectangle, ALL sides equal; circle = no corners, one curve. Tilting never changes a shape's name!

gameMission.title: The Shape City Site Inspection
gameMission.brief: Buildings are going up all over Shape City! Check every part — roofs, doors, windows, wheels — by counting sides and corners, and reject Ravi's mislabeled deliveries (especially his 'diamonds').

realLifeProject: Shape safari: walk one room with a parent and list 8 objects with their shapes. Bonus: find one square and PROVE it's also a rectangle by checking the rectangle rules aloud.

rememberIt.hook: Name 2D shapes by their sides and corners
rememberIt.unpack: Triangle 3, quadrilateral 4, pentagon 5, hexagon 6. Same number of sides and corners. A circle has no corners.

studentQuestions.q: Sides and corners - are they equal?
studentQuestions.a: For straight-sided shapes, yes - a 4-sided shape has 4 corners.
No - polygons have straight sides. A circle is one smooth curve.
